description: Voluntary postal survey on same-sex marriage. Yes won 61.6% with 79.5% participation. Tests social issue dynamics where campaigns mobilize rather than convert — remarkably stable opinion throughout.

key_events.description: Government announces postal survey after Senate blocks plebiscite bill (Aug 8).
High Court upholds legality of postal survey (Aug 24). Both campaigns formally launch.
Coalition for Marriage 'No' TV ad (mothers worried about school programs) generates backlash.
Hate speech incidents reported. Media covers psychological toll on LGBTQ community. Sympathy effect.
Result announced: 61.6% Yes, 79.5% participation. Parliament passes Marriage Amendment Act Dec 7.

calibration_notes: Tests social issue dynamics with real polling. Opinion moved only ~3-4 points despite intense campaigning. On mature social issues, most people have firm priors — campaigns mobilize rather than convert. Shy-No effect was small (~2-3pts). Negative campaigning tightened race temporarily but backlash roughly cancelled gains. Post-decision acceptance effect: support increases after implementation. High participation (79.5%) for voluntary postal survey shows salience drives turnout. Sources: Newspoll, Essential Research, Ipsos, Galaxy.
